steps.title: Expand the perfect square binomial
steps.content:
Apply the perfect square pattern to $(1.5x - 2.4)^2$ with $a = 1.5x$ and $b = 2.4$. Calculate each component:
$$(1.5x)^2 = 2.25x^2$$
$$-2(1.5x)(2.4) = -7.2x$$
$$(2.4)^2 = 5.76$$
Combine these terms:
$$(1.5x - 2.4)^2 = 2.25x^2 - 7.2x + 5.76$$

steps.title: Subtract the second expression and combine like terms
steps.content:
Now subtract $(5.2x^2 - 6.4)$ from the expanded form:
$$(2.25x^2 - 7.2x + 5.76) - (5.2x^2 - 6.4)$$
Distribute the negative sign and group like terms:
$$= 2.25x^2 - 5.2x^2 - 7.2x + 5.76 + 6.4$$
Simplify each group:
  • $x^2$ terms: $2.25 - 5.2 = -2.95$
  • $x$ terms: $-7.2x$ remains unchanged
  • Constants: $5.76 + 6.4 = 12.16$
$$= -2.95x^2 - 7.2x + 12.16$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
